name: rlua
description: High-level Lua 5.x bindings for Rust (deprecated - now a thin wrapper around mlua)

implementation.comment: Rust bindings to Lua C library via mlua; originally independent bindings before deprecation

latest-version.comment: Released July 20, 2024; now just re-exports mlua

paradigm.comment: Lua supports imperative, functional, and prototype-based OOP via metatables

closures.comment: Lua functions are closures with proper lexical scoping

pattern-matching.comment: Lua lacks native pattern matching; relies on if/elseif chains

error-handling.comment: Lua uses pcall/error for exceptions; rlua wraps these in Rust Result types

async-await.comment: No native async/await in Lua scripts; current versions delegate to mlua for async

iterators-generators.comment: Generic for loop with iterator functions; coroutines can act as generators

standard-library.comment: Standard libs: string, table, math, io, os, debug, coroutine, utf8

execution-strategy.comment: Standard Lua uses bytecode VM; rlua does not support LuaJIT (use mlua for JIT)

compilation-model.comment: Can load source at runtime or precompile to bytecode

hot-reloading.comment: Scripts can be reloaded via standard Lua mechanisms

repl-support.comment: Standard Lua includes REPL; rlua can evaluate strings interactively

debugging-support.comment: Hooks, stack inspection, tracebacks via debug library

source-maps.comment: Line number tracking and tracebacks available

startup-time.comment: Standard Lua has minimal startup overhead

execution-speed.comment: Standard Lua is fast bytecode; no JIT option in rlua (use mlua for LuaJIT)

memory-overhead.comment: Lua is designed for embedding with small footprint (~200KB core)

garbage-collection.comment: Lua 5.4 supports incremental and generational GC modes

pure-rust.comment: Bindings to Lua C library (vendored or system)

api-safety.comment: Original rlua design focused on safety; now delegates to mlua's safe API

idiomatic-api.comment: Good Rust patterns with traits (ToLua/FromLua, UserData); slightly dated compared to mlua's IntoLua

serde-integration.comment: Via mlua's serde support in v0.20+

async-compatible.comment: Via mlua dependency in v0.20+; original rlua was sync-only

function-registration.comment: UserData trait, UserDataMethods builder, closure registration

type-mapping.comment: ToLua/FromLua traits (aliased to mlua's IntoLua/FromLua in v0.20+)

no-std-compatible.comment: Requires std for Lua C integration

wasm-compatible.comment: Via mlua in v0.20+; supports wasm32-unknown-emscripten

blank-slate-mode.comment: Can create Lua state without standard libraries via StdLib configuration

memory-sandboxing.comment: Scripts cannot access arbitrary host memory

memory-limits.comment: Memory limits available via mlua in v0.20+

syscall-restrictions.comment: Can omit io/os libraries via StdLib flags

capability-security.comment: StdLib flags control which libraries are available

default-deny.comment: By default includes standard libraries; must explicitly restrict

share-data.comment: UserData for references, regular conversion clones

operator-overloading.comment: MetaMethod for __add, __sub, __index, etc. via Lua metatables

method-syntax.comment: UserDataMethods registers methods callable with colon syntax

callbacks-events.comment: Functions can be stored and called back from Rust

github-stars.comment: Approximate as of January 2026; repository is archived

crates-io-downloads.comment: Historical downloads; ~3.6k monthly as of Jan 2026 due to deprecated status

package-manager.comment: Can work with LuaRocks ecosystem for Lua packages

editor-support.comment: Lua has mature tooling: lua-language-server LSP, VS Code extensions

example-projects.comment: Was popular in Amethyst game engine ecosystem; most projects now use mlua

maintenance-status.comment: Repository archived September 2025; deprecated in favor of mlua

documentation-quality.comment: 0% documented per docs.rs; relies on mlua docs now

maturity.comment: Stable API but deprecated; users should migrate to mlua

primary-use-cases.comment: Same use cases as Lua; users should migrate to mlua for new projects
